Mini Requirements

Problem

Users currently have no structured way to capture product requirements. Teams fall back to ad-hoc notes which lose context and rationale over time.

Who experiences this?

Product managers and engineering leads who need to hand off feature context to implementation teams.

Goals

  • Clear problem statement — measured by: spec author can produce 02_spec without follow-up questions.
  • Explicit non-goals — measured by: zero scope-creep incidents in subsequent spec reviews.

Solution Direction

Introduce a tiered requirements template (Tier 1/2/3) that scales with feature complexity. The acid test: a product designer can evaluate design options from the doc alone.

Open Questions

  1. Should rollout be eager or lazy?
